title: Spectrophotometric determination of tungsten(VI) using 3-hydroxy-6-methyl-2-( 4' -tolyl)-4-oxo-4H-1-benzopyran
author: Joginder Rohilla
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Kurukshetra University, Kurukshetra-136 119, Haryana, India 

 Department of Chemistry, S. D. College, Ambala Cantt.-133 001, Haryana, India 

  E-mail  : jogi001s@gmail.com, jogi001@rediffmail.com 

  Manuscript received 16 April 2008, revised 15 December 2008, accepted 19 December 2008  

 A new, simple, sensitive and selective spectrophotometric method is developed for the trace determination of tungsten. A light yellow (1 : 2) complex of tungsten(VI) is formed with 3-hydroxy-6-methyl-1-(4 ' -tolyl)-4-oxo-4 H -1- benzopyran (HMTB) in perchloric acid medium. The coloured species is extracted into chloroform and absorbance is measured at 396-402 nm. The method obeys Beer's law in the range 0-9.5 µg W vI  ml -1 , with a molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity of 1.43 x 10 5  dm 3  mol -1  cm -1  and 0.0013 µg W VI  cm -1 , respectively at 398 nm. The method is free from the Interference of a large number of elements and has been applied for the determination of tungsten in a variety of samples with Satisfactory accuracy.
